content: read reviews/539.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/539.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/539.md.

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/539.lean — Erdős Problem 539

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 539`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/539](https://www.erdosproblems.com/539)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defines h(n), gcd-quotient sets, known bounds by Erdős-Szemerédi and Freiman-Lev).
- [x] Optional bibliography: not present, but the docstring names the relevant authors (Erdős-Szemerédi, Freiman-Lev) inline.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos539 ... end Erdos539`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos539`.
- [x] `open Finset` appears **before** the namespace block (line 30, before line 32).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `gcdQuotientSet` has a LaTeX docstring: `/-- The set of gcd-quotients ... -/`.
- [x] Definition is computable; `noncomputable` not needed.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Function name uses `lowerCamelCase`: `gcdQuotientSet`.
- N/A: No local notation needed; no types/Props defined.
- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Both declarations use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_539` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**:
  - `erdos_539`: `@[category research solved, AMS 11]`
  - `erdos_539.variants.upper_bound`: `@[category research solved, AMS 11]`
- [x] Both theorems have docstrings.
- [x] Docstrings reference the source results: "Erdős-Szemerédi" (lower bound) and "Freiman-Lev" (upper bound).

### `answer()` usage

The original problem describes known proved bounds on the size of gcd-quotient sets. The formalization states these bounds as direct assertions (Case 3 — direct assertion / "Prove that ...").

- [x] No `answer()` wrapper used — appropriate for Case 3 (direct assertions about established bounds).
- [x] Quantifiers are not placed as theorem parameters; they correctly appear in the theorem body (`∀ A : Finset ℕ, ...` and `∀ n : ℕ, ...`).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
- [x] Category value is valid: `research solved` (known results by Erdős-Szemerédi and Freiman-Lev, not yet formally verified).
- N/A: No `research open` theorems to check for `sorry` consistency.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed: `11` (Number theory).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single number — trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Both proofs use the multi-line `by\n  sorry` form (acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- N/A: File is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches included for research problems.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variant named `erdos_539.variants.upper_bound` (correct naming convention).
- [x] Variant lives in the **same file** as the main theorem.
- [x] Variant has its own `@[category ..., AMS ...]` attributes.
- [x] Variant has a docstring explaining it captures the upper bound (Freiman-Lev).

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `≤`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] All lines reasonably short (<100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linter pass not verified in this review.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (modulo build verification)

The file is clean and well-structured. All checklist items are satisfied. The formalization correctly captures both the Erdős-Szemerédi lower bound and the Freiman-Lev upper bound as separate theorems with appropriate `research solved` categorization. The helper definition `gcdQuotientSet` is well-documented and correctly computable. No issues found.

content.text: The review passed (**Result: PASS**). No changes needed.
